data.content: Need to upgrade MSTest to v4 (which has `ThrowsExactly`) and enable implicit usings. Let me fix the project:

arguments.old_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
There are two issues:
1. `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` needs `using System;` in both files
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` doesn't exist in MSTest 3.6.3 - it was introduced in MSTest v4. Let me check what version we have.

The project uses MSTest 3.6.3. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` was introduced in MSTest 4.x. For MSTest 3.x, I should use `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` instead, OR I need to upgrade the MSTest packages.

Wait, the user asked me to modernize to use `Assert.ThrowsExactly`. Let me check if I should upgrade the packages or use what's available. 

Since the user specifically wants `Assert.ThrowsExactly`, I should upgrade to MSTest v4. I also need to check if `IsInstanceOfType<T>` is available in the current version, and add the missing `using System;` directive to both files.

I'll enable `ImplicitUsings` in the project file and upgrade the MSTest packages to v4 to get access to the newer assertion methods.

<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the modernizations applied:

**Changes made:**

| Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|
| `public class` | `public sealed class` |
| `TestContext?` (nullable) | `required TestContext { get; init; }` |
| `[TestInitialize]` for simple setup | Field initializer `= new()` |
| `[ExpectedException]` | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` |
| Swapped `Assert.AreEqual` args | Correct order: `(expected, actual)` |
| Vague test names (`Test1`) | Descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` names |
| Hard cast + `Assert.IsTrue` | `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>()` + `StringAssert.Contains` |
| Manual `foreach` loop test data | `[DataRow]` attributes with `DisplayName` |

Also upgraded MSTest from 3.6.3 → 3.8.3 and enabled `ImplicitUsings`.
